purpose: Maps Minsky cycle phases to observable credit vol properties for regime identification and timing of credit vol repricing.
components: Hedge phase: Credit vol is mean-reverting, spread shocks quickly absorbed, leverage moderate
Speculative phase: Credit vol becomes path-dependent, spread shocks persist longer, leverage elevated
Ponzi phase: Credit vol becomes explosive, small triggers produce outsized spread moves, leverage extreme
HY-IG spread differential thresholds: <280bp = Hedge/early Speculative; 280-350bp = late Speculative; >350-400bp = approaching Ponzi transition
current_assessment: Current HY-IG spread at ~280-320bp places us in late Speculative phase, below the 350-400bp threshold for serious repositioning. Supporting indicators (leverage ratios up 0.3-0.5x, coverage ratios down 0.5-1.0x, upgrade/downgrade ratio below 1.0 for five quarters) consistent with late-cycle deterioration but not uniquely identifying a regime transition.
limitations: Similar metrics deteriorated in 2015-2016 and 2019 without producing a credit cycle turn
Claimed zero false positives across seven episodes requires independent validation
Real-time leverage data has reporting lag
Does not incorporate private credit dynamics
